content.text: Submissions has no `video_url`? Let me check US-045's migration.

content.content:
1	-- US-045 — RPC backing the submit-video edge function.
2	--
3	-- Contract: public.submit_video_rpc(
4	--   p_application_id uuid,
5	--   p_creator_id uuid,
6	--   p_video_url text,
7	--   p_platform public.platform,
8	--   p_external_id text default null,
9	--   p_oembed jsonb default null
10	-- ) returns jsonb.
11	--
12	-- On success: {"submission_id": "<uuid>"}.
13	-- On conflict: {"error": "APPLICATION_NOT_FOUND" | "APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED" | "SUBMISSION_EXISTS"}.
14	--
15	-- Concurrency: locks the application row FOR UPDATE so a concurrent
16	-- approval flip or a duplicate-submit attempt serialises through this
17	-- gate. The partial unique index `submissions_open_uniq` on
18	-- (application_id) WHERE status IN ('pending','approved') is the DB-level
19	-- guarantee; the explicit pre-check yields a clean 409 instead of relying
20	-- on unique_violation diagnostics.
21	--
22	-- Caller responsibilities (NOT enforced here):
23	--   - URL shape validation (TikTok / Instagram regex)
24	--   - oEmbed liveness check
25	--   - post_condition affirmations completeness
26	--
27	-- Auth: revoked from anon/authenticated/public. Only service_role (used
28	-- by the edge function) may execute.
29	
30	create or replace function public.submit_video_rpc(
31	  p_application_id uuid,
32	  p_creator_id uuid,
33	  p_video_url text,
34	  p_platform public.platform,
35	  p_external_id text default null,
36	  p_oembed jsonb default null
37	) returns jsonb
38	language plpgsql
39	set search_path = ''
40	as $$
41	declare
42	  v_creator_id uuid;
43	  v_status public.application_status;
44	  v_submission_id uuid;
45	begin
46	  select a.creator_id, a.status
47	    into v_creator_id, v_status
48	    from public.applications a
49	   where a.id = p_application_id
50	     for update;
51	
52	  if not found or v_creator_id <> p_creator_id then
53	    return jsonb_build_object('error', 'APPLICATION_NOT_FOUND');
54	  end if;
55	
56	  if v_status <> 'approved'::public.application_status then
57	    return jsonb_build_object('error', 'APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED');
58	  end if;
59	
60	  if exists (
61	    select 1
62	      from public.submissions s
63	     where s.application_id = p_application_id
64	       and s.status in (
65	         'pending'::public.submission_status,
66	         'approved'::public.submission_status
67	       )
68	  ) then
69	    return jsonb_build_object('error', 'SUBMISSION_EXISTS');
70	  end if;
71	
72	  insert into public.submissions (application_id, status)
73	  values (p_application_id, 'pending'::public.submission_status)
74	  returning id into v_submission_id;
75	
76	  insert into public.submission_videos (
77	    submission_id, platform, url, external_id, oembed_cached, last_validated_at, sort_order
78	  ) values (
79	    v_submission_id, p_platform, p_video_url, p_external_id, p_oembed, now(), 0
80	  );
81	
82	  return jsonb_build_object('submission_id', v_submission_id);
83	exception
84	  when unique_violation then
85	    return jsonb_build_object('error', 'SUBMISSION_EXISTS');
86	end;
87	$$;
88	
89	revoke all on function public.submit_video_rpc(uuid, uuid, text, public.platform, text, jsonb) from public;
90	revoke all on function public.submit_video_rpc(uuid, uuid, text, public.platform, text, jsonb) from anon, authenticated;
91	grant execute on function public.submit_video_rpc(uuid, uuid, text, public.platform, text, jsonb) to service_role;
92	
93	comment on function public.submit_video_rpc(uuid, uuid, text, public.platform, text, jsonb) is
94	  'US-045 submit-video RPC. Called only by the submit-video edge function via service_role. Acquires FOR UPDATE on the application row to serialise duplicate submits and approval-flip races; returns jsonb {submission_id} or {error}.';
95

input.name: us_047_list_my_submissions_rpc
input.query:
-- US-047 — SECURITY DEFINER function `list_my_submissions()` that returns
-- the caller's submissions joined to applications + listings + users
-- (lister handle) + listing_versions (snapshot title) + submission_videos
-- (first video by sort_order). Mirrors the US-043 `list_my_applications`
-- pattern for the same reasons: a raw PostgREST embed would silently null
-- `users.username` (users RLS is self-only) and listing_versions rows for
-- closed/cancelled listings (listing_versions RLS is gated on listing
-- status). Ownership is enforced inside the function via
-- public.current_user_id() (JWT sub claim) traversed through
-- submissions → applications.creator_id.
--
-- Execute is granted to `authenticated` only; anon and service_role are
-- not granted.

create or replace function public.list_my_submissions()
returns table (
  id               uuid,
  status           public.submission_status,
  created_at       timestamptz,
  application_id   uuid,
  listing_id       uuid,
  listing_title    text,
  lister_handle    text,
  version_title    text,
  video_url        text,
  video_platform   public.platform
)
language sql
security definer
stable
set search_path = ''
as $$
  select s.id,
         s.status,
         s.created_at,
         s.application_id,
         l.id as listing_id,
         l.title as listing_title,
         u.username::text as lister_handle,
         (lv.snapshot ->> 'title') as version_title,
         sv.url as video_url,
         sv.platform as video_platform
  from public.submissions s
  join public.applications a             on a.id = s.application_id
  left join public.listings l            on l.id = a.listing_id
  left join public.users u               on u.id = l.lister_id
  left join public.listing_versions lv   on lv.id = a.listing_version_id
  left join lateral (
    select url, platform
    from public.submission_videos
    where submission_id = s.id
    order by sort_order asc, id asc
    limit 1
  ) sv on true
  where a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
  order by s.created_at desc;
$$;

revoke all on function public.list_my_submissions() from public;
revoke all on function public.list_my_submissions() from anon;
grant execute on function public.list_my_submissions() to authenticated;

input.name: us_047_submissions_realtime_publication
input.query:
-- US-047 — add public.submissions to the supabase_realtime publication so
-- creators receive UPDATE events when a lister flips their submission to
-- approved or rejected. RLS is evaluated by the Realtime broker via
-- supabase.realtime.setAuth(marketify_jwt); submissions has no direct
-- creator_id column, so we rely on the `submissions_creator_rw` policy's
-- join-through-applications subquery to gate the stream (same as the
-- REST read path). Default replica identity (primary key) is sufficient
-- because the client only reads payload.new.id + payload.new.status.

alter publication supabase_realtime add table public.submissions;

input.content:
-- US-047 — SECURITY DEFINER function `list_my_submissions()` that returns
-- the caller's submissions joined to applications + listings + users
-- (lister handle) + listing_versions (snapshot title) + submission_videos
-- (first video by sort_order). Mirrors the US-043 `list_my_applications`
-- pattern for the same reasons: a raw PostgREST embed would silently null
-- `users.username` (users RLS is self-only) and listing_versions rows for
-- closed/cancelled listings (listing_versions RLS is gated on listing
-- status). Ownership is enforced inside the function via
-- public.current_user_id() (JWT sub claim) traversed through
-- submissions → applications.creator_id.
--
-- Execute is granted to `authenticated` only; anon and service_role are
-- not granted.

create or replace function public.list_my_submissions()
returns table (
  id               uuid,
  status           public.submission_status,
  created_at       timestamptz,
  application_id   uuid,
  listing_id       uuid,
  listing_title    text,
  lister_handle    text,
  version_title    text,
  video_url        text,
  video_platform   public.platform
)
language sql
security definer
stable
set search_path = ''
as $$
  select s.id,
         s.status,
         s.created_at,
         s.application_id,
         l.id as listing_id,
         l.title as listing_title,
         u.username::text as lister_handle,
         (lv.snapshot ->> 'title') as version_title,
         sv.url as video_url,
         sv.platform as video_platform
  from public.submissions s
  join public.applications a             on a.id = s.application_id
  left join public.listings l            on l.id = a.listing_id
  left join public.users u               on u.id = l.lister_id
  left join public.listing_versions lv   on lv.id = a.listing_version_id
  left join lateral (
    select url, platform
    from public.submission_videos
    where submission_id = s.id
    order by sort_order asc, id asc
    limit 1
  ) sv on true
  where a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
  order by s.created_at desc;
$$;

revoke all on function public.list_my_submissions() from public;
revoke all on function public.list_my_submissions() from anon;
grant execute on function public.list_my_submissions() to authenticated;

input.content:
-- US-047 — add public.submissions to the supabase_realtime publication so
-- creators receive UPDATE events when a lister flips their submission to
-- approved or rejected. RLS is evaluated by the Realtime broker via
-- supabase.realtime.setAuth(marketify_jwt); submissions has no direct
-- creator_id column, so we rely on the `submissions_creator_rw` policy's
-- join-through-applications subquery to gate the stream (same as the
-- REST read path). Default replica identity (primary key) is sufficient
-- because the client only reads payload.new.id + payload.new.status.

alter publication supabase_realtime add table public.submissions;

lastPrompt: # Marketify — Ralph Agent Instructions  You are an autonomous coding agent building **Marketify**, a mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers (Expo + React Native + Supabase). Eac…

content.text: Now let me check StatusPill to confirm the status prop accepts submission values.

content.content: The file /Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(creator)/submissions.tsx has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +import { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useState } from 'react';
-// US-047 replaces the placeholder with the submissions list.
+// US-047 — Creator "My Submissions" tab. Mirrors the US-043 applications
+// screen: calls `list_my_submissions` (SECURITY DEFINER, scoped to the
+// caller's applications) and renders three segments matching the
+// submission_status enum (pending / approved / rejected). Press routes
+// to the listing detail so creators can re-read the brief; submissions
+// have no dedicated detail screen in v1.
+// Realtime: subscribes to UPDATE events on public.submissions. The
+// submissions table has no direct creator_id column, so we omit the
+// server-side filter and let the broker's RLS evaluation
+// update lands in the companion migration
+// session the literal dev token is rejected by the broker (Codebase
+// Pattern #122) — empty-state screenshots verify wiring only.
+// Spec gap: AC text calls the file `submitted.tsx`, but the TabBar
+// route established in US-031 is `/(creator)/submissions`. Renaming the
+// file would invalidate the established routing convention, so the file
+// name stays as `submissions.tsx` while the header renders "Submitted"
+const SEGMENTS: readonly { key: SubmissionStatus; label: string }[] = [
+function SegmentButton({ label, selected, onPress, testID }: SegmentButtonProps) {

input.query:
-- Smoke: verify list_my_submissions returns flat rows scoped to the caller.
-- Pick a seed creator user and verify the function returns rows under that JWT context.
SELECT c.user_id, u.username, count(s.id) AS submission_count
FROM public.creator_profiles c
JOIN public.users u ON u.id = c.user_id
LEFT JOIN public.applications a ON a.creator_id = c.user_id
LEFT JOIN public.submissions s ON s.application_id = a.id
GROUP BY c.user_id, u.username
HAVING count(s.id) > 0
LIMIT 3;

input.prompt:
You're reviewing **US-047: My Submissions tab (creator)** for Marketify before commit.

## Acceptance criteria (from scripts/ralph/prd.json)
- `app/(creator)/submitted.tsx` mirrors the applied tab structure for submissions
- Realtime subscription on submissions WHERE creator_user_id = me
- Mobile-mcp verification: screenshot each segment
- Commit

**Scope note**: the existing route file is `app/(creator)/submissions.tsx` (established in US-031 where the TabBar routes to `/(creator)/submissions`). Renaming to `submitted.tsx` would break the routing convention. The file was kept as `submissions.tsx` — this is documented as a Spec gap in the file header.

## Diff scope
Review these files only:
1. `supabase/migrations/20260418050000_us_047_list_my_submissions_rpc.sql` — SECURITY DEFINER RPC returning caller-scoped submission rows (mirrors `list_my_applications` from US-043)
2. `supabase/migrations/20260418050100_us_047_submissions_realtime_publication.sql` — adds `public.submissions` to `supabase_realtime`
3. `src/types/supabase.ts` — regenerated types; only adds `list_my_submissions` function signature + submission tables now on the realtime publication
4. `app/(creator)/submissions.tsx` — rewritten from TabPlaceholder stub into the real screen

## Conventions to check against
- `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` — the US-043/US-044 sibling screen this should mirror in structure and realtime wiring
- `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql` — the RPC pattern this mirrors
- `progress.txt` "Codebase Patterns" section (top of file) — especially:
  - Pattern #39 (RLS helpers `current_user_id()`, `current_user_role()`, `app_role` JWT claim)
  - Pattern #42 (trigger/function search_path hardening)
  - Pattern #105 (`supabase.realtime.setAuth` for websocket auth)
  - Pattern #82/#106 (useRef mirror of state to avoid stale-closure in subscription callbacks)
  - Pattern #122 (DevRolePicker dev token rejected by Realtime broker; empty-state screenshots verify wiring only)

## Things to pay special attention to
1. **RPC ownership check**: does the WHERE clause correctly scope to the caller's applications? submissions has no direct `creator_id`, so the join-through-applications is the ownership boundary. Does it fail closed when `current_user_id()` returns NULL?
2. **Realtime filter absence**: I deliberately omitted the `filter: 'creator_id=eq.<id>'` param because submissions has no such column. RLS is the security boundary — is that correct, or is there a better single-column filter I'm missing?
3. **lateral join for video_url**: does it correctly pick the lowest-sort_order video per submission, and does it not fan out rows when there are multiple videos attached?
4. **useEffect dependencies** in the realtime subscription: I intentionally did NOT include `showToast` like the applications screen does, because US-047 AC doesn't specify a toast. Is there any other stale-closure hazard?
5. **StatusPill status typing**: `row.status` is `submission_status` (pending/approved/rejected). `StatusPillStatus` is `'pending' | 'approved' | 'rejected' | 'cancelled'`. The assignment widens cleanly, but confirm.
6. **Migrations**: are they idempotent-safe? (The RPC uses `create or replace`, the publication `alter publication ... add table` is NOT idempotent — a re-apply would fail if the table is already in the publication. Same pattern as US-044, so it's consistent but worth flagging.)

Report high/medium severity issues only. Skip style nits. Under 400 words.

content.text:
I now have all the information needed. Here is the review.

---

Reviewing the four files for US-047 against the US-043/US-044 reference implementation and the conventions in progress.txt.

## Critical

None.

## Important

**1. NULL `current_user_id()` does not fail closed — it returns ALL submissions (Confidence: 95)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260418050000_us_047_list_my_submissions_rpc.sql`, line 55.

The WHERE clause is:

```sql
where a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
```

`current_user_id()` returns `NULL` when `auth.jwt()` has no `sub` claim (unauthenticated or malformed token). In SQL, `a.creator_id = NULL` evaluates to `UNKNOWN`, which is falsy — so **no rows are returned** when the caller is unauthenticated. This is fail-closed; the behavior is correct.

This was one of the explicitly flagged concerns but the conclusion is: it is safe. No fix needed.

**2. Realtime publication migration is not idempotent (Confidence: 88)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260418050100_us_047_submissions_realtime_publication.sql`, line 10.

```sql
alter publication supabase_realtime add table public.submissions;
```

`ALTER PUBLICATION ... ADD TABLE` will throw `ERROR: relation "submissions" is already member of publication "supabase_realtime"` if re-applied. There is no `IF NOT EXISTS` guard. This is consistent with the US-044 migration for `applications` and is therefore an accepted project pattern, but it means a migration replay (e.g., `supabase db reset`) on a database where this table is already in the publication will fail. Since `supabase db reset` re-runs all migrations from scratch on a fresh schema the table cannot pre-exist, so in practice this is only a problem if someone manually adds the table or runs the migration file twice without a reset. Flag it as a known non-idempotency to document, consistent with how US-044 was noted.

No code change is required given the established pattern, but it is worth logging as a known gap.

**3. Lateral join is correct; no fan-out risk (Confidence: 100)**

File: `20260418050000_us_047_list_my_submissions_rpc.sql`, lines 48-54.

The `left join lateral (... limit 1) sv on true` correctly emits at most one video row per submission. `on true` means submissions with zero videos get a NULL `sv` row rather than being dropped. No fan-out is possible.

**4. `useRef` stale-closure pattern is correctly applied (Confidence: 100)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(creator)/submissions.tsx`, lines 111-114, 159.

`rowsRef` is synced in a `useEffect` and read inside the subscription callback. The callback only reads `rowsRef.current` (never the `rows` state variable directly), which matches Pattern #82/#106. The `useEffect` dependency array for the subscription (line 174) contains only `[userId]` — omitting `showToast` is correct here because there is no toast in this screen, so there is no stale reference hazard from that omission. Clean.

**5. `StatusPillStatus` assignment widens cleanly (Confidence: 100)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(creator)/submissions.tsx`, line 246.

```ts
const pillStatus: StatusPillStatus = row.status;
```

`submission_status` is `'pending' | 'approved' | 'rejected'`, which is a strict subset of `StatusPillStatus` (`'pending' | 'approved' | 'rejected' | 'cancelled'`). The assignment is valid without a cast. No issue.

**6. `router.push` with null `listing_id` is a latent crash risk (Confidence: 85)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(creator)/submissions.tsx`, line 250.

```ts
router.push(`/(creator)/listing/${row.listing_id}`);
```

`listing_id` is typed as `string | null` (the RPC uses `left join public.listings`, so if the listing row was hard-deleted `listing_id` would be null). The RPC column is `l.id as listing_id`, which is from a `left join` — the TypeScript return type likely reflects `string | null`. Pressing a card for an orphaned submission would route to `/(creator)/listing/null`, which will 404 or crash the detail screen. The applications screen has the same pattern on its `router.push` call, so this is a pre-existing pattern and not introduced by this PR, but it is worth noting since submissions are more likely to outlive their parent listing than applications are. No fix required to match the established pattern; flag as a known gap.

---

**Summary**: No blocking issues. The RPC is structurally correct and fails closed. The lateral join is safe. The realtime wiring matches the US-044 pattern exactly. The only standing gap is the non-idempotent `ALTER PUBLICATION` statement, which is a known project-wide pattern rather than a regression in this PR.

     - SECURITY DEFINER function mirroring US-043's `list_my_applications` pattern
     - Returns flat rows joining submissions → applications → listings → users → listing_versions + lateral-joined submission_videos
     ```sql
     create or replace function public.list_my_submissions()
     returns table (
       id uuid, status public.submission_status, created_at timestamptz,
       application_id uuid, listing_id uuid, listing_title text,
       lister_handle text, version_title text,
       video_url text, video_platform public.platform
     )
     language sql security definer stable
     set search_path = ''
     as $
       select s.id, s.status, s.created_at, s.application_id,
              l.id as listing_id, l.title as listing_title,
              u.username::text as lister_handle,
              (lv.snapshot ->> 'title') as version_title,
              sv.url as video_url, sv.platform as video_platform
       from public.submissions s
       join public.applications a on a.id = s.application_id
       left join public.listings l on l.id = a.listing_id
       left join public.users u on u.id = l.lister_id
       left join public.listing_versions lv on lv.id = a.listing_version_id
     ```

     - Spec gap documented: kept filename `submissions.tsx` not `submitted.tsx` because US-031 TabBar routes to `/(creator)/submissions`
     - Key subscription block:
     ```ts
     useEffect(() => {
       if (!userId) return;
       const token = getCachedToken();
       if (!token) return;
       let cancelled = false;
       let activeChannel: ReturnType<typeof supabase.channel> | null = null;
       void supabase.realtime.setAuth(token).then(() => {
         if (cancelled) return;
         activeChannel = supabase
           .channel(`creator-submissions-${userId}`)
           .on('postgres_changes',
             { event: 'UPDATE', schema: 'public', table: 'submissions' },
             (payload) => {
               const next = payload.new as { id?: string; status?: SubmissionStatus };
               if (!next.id || !next.status) return;
               const prior = rowsRef.current.find((r) => r.id === next.id);
               if (!prior) return;
               if (prior.status === next.status) return;
               const nextStatus = next.status;
               setRows((cur) => cur.map((r) => (r.id === next.id ? { ...r, status: nextStatus } : r)));
             })
           .subscribe();
       });
       return () => {
         cancelled = true;
         if (activeChannel) void supabase.removeChannel(activeChannel);
       };
     }, [userId]);
     ```

file.content:
-- US-047 — add public.submissions to the supabase_realtime publication so
-- creators receive UPDATE events when a lister flips their submission to
-- approved or rejected. RLS is evaluated by the Realtime broker via
-- supabase.realtime.setAuth(marketify_jwt); submissions has no direct
-- creator_id column, so we rely on the `submissions_creator_rw` policy's
-- join-through-applications subquery to gate the stream (same as the
-- REST read path). Default replica identity (primary key) is sufficient
-- because the client only reads payload.new.id + payload.new.status.

alter publication supabase_realtime add table public.submissions;

-- US-047 — SECURITY DEFINER function `list_my_submissions()` that returns
-- the caller's submissions joined to applications + listings + users
-- (lister handle) + listing_versions (snapshot title) + submission_videos
-- (first video by sort_order). Mirrors the US-043 `list_my_applications`
-- pattern for the same reasons: a raw PostgREST embed would silently null
-- `users.username` (users RLS is self-only) and listing_versions rows for
-- closed/cancelled listings (listing_versions RLS is gated on listing
-- status). Ownership is enforced inside the function via
-- public.current_user_id() (JWT sub claim) traversed through
-- submissions → applications.creator_id.
--
-- Execute is granted to `authenticated` only; anon and service_role are
-- not granted.

create or replace function public.list_my_submissions()
returns table (
  id               uuid,
  status           public.submission_status,
  created_at       timestamptz,
  application_id   uuid,
  listing_id       uuid,
  listing_title    text,
  lister_handle    text,
  version_title    text,
  video_url        text,
  video_platform   public.platform
)
language sql
security definer
stable
set search_path = ''
as $$
  select s.id,
         s.status,
         s.created_at,
         s.application_id,
         l.id as listing_id,
         l.title as listing_title,
         u.username::text as lister_handle,
         (lv.snapshot ->> 'title') as version_title,
         sv.url as video_url,
         sv.platform as video_platform
  from public.submissions s
  join public.applications a             on a.id = s.application_id
  left join public.listings l            on l.id = a.listing_id
  left join public.users u               on u.id = l.lister_id
  left join public.listing_versions lv   on lv.id = a.listing_version_id
  left join lateral (
    select url, platform
    from public.submission_videos
    where submission_id = s.id
    order by sort_order asc, id asc
    limit 1
  ) sv on true
  where a.creator_id = public.current_user_id()
  order by s.created_at desc;
$$;

revoke all on function public.list_my_submissions() from public;
revoke all on function public.list_my_submissions() from anon;
grant execute on function public.list_my_submissions() to authenticated;

# Marketify — Ralph Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent building **Marketify**, a mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers (Expo + React Native + Supabase). Each invocation is a fresh Claude Code instance with no prior memory. Every persistent decision lives in git history, `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`, and `scripts/ralph/prd.json`.

---

## Authoritative specs (read these for any ambiguity)

| File | What it defines |
|---|---|
| `docs/product-plan.md` | Canonical product, 12-step build sequence, scope cuts |
| `docs/design.md` | Visual language, component inventory, screen catalog, motion |
| `docs/tech-architecture.md` | DB schema (§4.7), RLS, triggers, Apify integration (§3), edge-function spec, eligibility engine |

If a story is ambiguous, the specs win. Do **not** invent product behavior the docs don't sanction. If something is genuinely missing, append a `Spec gap:` note to `progress.txt` and pick the simplest interpretation that aligns with the docs.

---

## The loop you run, every iteration

1. **Orient.** Read `scripts/ralph/prd.json` and `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`. Pay special attention to the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt` — it consolidates everything previous iterations learned.
2. **Branch.** Make sure you're on the branch named in `prd.json.branchName`. If it doesn't exist, create it from `main`.
3. **Pick one story.** Lowest `priority` number where `passes: false`. Work on **exactly one** story per iteration.
4. **Research before coding.** If understanding the story requires reading >3 files, **delegate to the Explore subagent** (`subagent_type: "Explore"`) so your own context stays clean for implementation. Pass it the story plus pointers into the docs.
5. **Implement.** Make the minimal change set that satisfies every acceptance criterion. Match existing conventions. Don't refactor adjacent code.
6. **Quality gates** (run in this order, stop on first failure and fix before continuing):
   - `npx tsc --noEmit` — must pass with zero errors
   - `npx expo lint` — must pass (warnings allowed only if pre-existing in main)
   - Migration sanity: any new SQL must apply cleanly via Supabase MCP without manual fix-up
7. **Code review (mandatory before commit).** Spawn the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent with the diff scope, the story acceptance criteria, and the relevant doc sections. Address every issue rated medium or higher. Re-run quality gates after fixes.
8. **Mobile verification (mandatory for any UI story).** Use `mobile-mcp` to:
   - Launch the app on the iOS simulator (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_launch_app`)
   - Navigate to the screen the story touches
   - Exercise the golden path
   - Take a screenshot (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_take_screenshot`) and reference it in the progress note
   - If the simulator is unavailable, **do not silently skip** — set `passes: false`, write a `notes` entry on the story explaining the blocker, and move to the next story.
9. **Commit.** Stage everything you changed (no `git add -A` blanket — list paths). Commit message format:
   ```
   feat: [US-XXX] Story title
   ```
10. **Mark done.** In `prd.json`, set `passes: true` for the story you just finished. Append a dated entry to `progress.txt` (format below).
11. **Stop check.** If every story has `passes: true`, your final response must contain `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` and nothing else useful. Otherwise end normally — the next iteration picks up.

---

## MCP servers — use these instead of CLI wherever possible

### Supabase (`mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__*`) — required for ALL database work
- `apply_migration` — every schema change. Migrations are append-only and named after the story (e.g. `us_006_metric_snapshots_denorm_trigger`).
- `execute_sql` — read-only queries, RLS verification, seed data inspection.
- `list_tables`, `list_migrations`, `list_extensions` — orient before changing things.
- `generate_typescript_types` — regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts` after every migration. Commit the regenerated file with the migration.
- `deploy_edge_function`, `get_edge_function`, `list_edge_functions` — for `apify-core`, `auth-*`, `metrics-refresh`, the webhook receiver, etc.
- `get_advisors` — run with `type: "security"` after any schema or RLS change. Address every error-level advisor before committing.
- `get_logs` — when an edge function or query misbehaves.

Do **not** use the local `supabase` CLI for migrations or queries. The MCP is the source of truth so iterations stay consistent.

### Mobile (`mcp__mobile-mcp__*`) — required for any UI verification
- `mobile_list_available_devices` then `mobile_launch_app` to boot the app.
- `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` + `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` to drive flows.
- `mobile_take_screenshot` after each meaningful state — name screenshots `us-XXX-state.png` and store under `scripts/ralph/screenshots/`.
- Use `mobile_type_keys` rather than tapping virtual keys when filling text inputs.

### Context7 (`mcp__plugin_context7_context7__*`) — for any library docs
Use **before** writing non-trivial integration code with Expo, Reanimated, NativeWind, Supabase JS, React Navigation, etc. Your training data may be stale. `resolve-library-id` then `query-docs`.

### Apify
The API key is in `.env` as `APIFY_KEY`. Always read it at runtime via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` (or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` inside edge functions). **Never** paste the key into source. Actor catalog and input schemas live in `docs/tech-architecture.md` §3.

---

## Subagent delegation — preserve your context

You will burn out your own context window if you try to do everything in-line. Delegate aggressively:

| When | Subagent | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Need to read >3 files to understand the codebase | `Explore` | Returns a focused summary instead of dumping raw file contents into your context |
| Before every commit | `feature-dev:code-reviewer` | Independent review against acceptance criteria + doc sections |
| Implementation will touch >5 files (e.g. wiring a new edge function end-to-end) | `feature-dev:code-architect` first, then implement yourself | Plan once, execute once |
| Need to research how something works before changing it | `feature-dev:code-explorer` | Maps the existing layers without polluting your context |
| Open-ended search across the repo | `general-purpose` | Iterates until it finds the answer |

When briefing a subagent, give it the story ID, acceptance criteria, and a pointer to the relevant `docs/*.md` section. Do **not** tell it "based on your findings, implement" — synthesize the result yourself.

---

## Project quality conventions (these become permanent — additions go in `progress.txt` Codebase Patterns)

- **TypeScript everywhere.** No `any` without a `// eslint-disable-next-line` and a comment explaining why. Generated Supabase types are imported from `src/types/supabase.ts`.
- **Migrations are append-only.** Never edit a previously applied migration. If you need to change something, write a new migration that alters the prior state.
- **Edge functions** live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`. Each one has a one-paragraph header comment naming the contract (request shape → response shape) and the auth requirement.
- **RLS is mandatory** on every table that holds user data. After applying any RLS migration, run `get_advisors({type: "security"})` and fix every finding before committing.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific composites in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji** anywhere in the product UI (per `docs/design.md` — Lucide icons only).
- **No payments code** in v1 — `price_cents` is display-only.
- **Dual-role accounts** are out of scope for v1. One role per account.

---

## Progress log format (`progress.txt`)

Append (never replace) using this format:

```
## YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM — US-XXX
- What was implemented (1–3 bullets, concrete)
- Files changed (bulleted list)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ / mobile-mcp ✓
- Code-reviewer verdict: [approved | issues fixed in this commit]
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered
  - Gotchas
  - Useful pointers
---
```

If a learning is **general and reusable across stories**, also promote it into the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt`. That section is what every future iteration leans on first — keep it tight, deduplicated, and signal-only.

---

## Stop condition

After updating `prd.json`, count remaining `passes: false` stories. If zero, your last line in this response must be exactly:

```
<promise>COMPLETE</promise>
```

Otherwise end normally and let the loop spawn the next iteration.

---

## Hard rules (do not violate)

- **One story per iteration.** Don't peek ahead.
- **Never commit broken code.** A failing typecheck means you don't commit, you don't mark `passes: true`, you write a `notes` entry, and the next iteration retries.
- **Never skip the code-reviewer subagent.** It is the second pair of eyes that keeps quality from drifting across iterations.
- **Never skip mobile verification on UI stories.** A screen "looking right in the JSX" is not the same as it actually rendering correctly.
- **Never read `.env` contents into the conversation.** Reference variable names only.
- **Never edit `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, or `docs/tech-architecture.md`** unless a story explicitly says to. They are specs, not work product.
- **Never invent stories** or add new ones to `prd.json` mid-loop. If you discover a missing piece, append it as a `Spec gap:` line in `progress.txt` so the human can decide.

## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

1	# Ralph Progress Log — Marketify
2	Started: (will be replaced on first run)
3	---
4	
5	## Codebase Patterns
6	*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*
7	
8	- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
9	- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
10	- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
11	- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
12	- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
13	- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
14	- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
15	- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.
16	- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
17	- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
18	- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
19	- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
20	- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
21	- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
22	- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
23	- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
24	- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
25	- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
26	- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
27	- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
28	- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
29	- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
30	- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
31	- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
32	- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
33	- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
34	- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
35	- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
36	- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
37	- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
38	- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
39	- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. **Note the `app_role` claim (not `role`)** — the bare `role` claim is reserved by PostgREST for the Postgres DB role (always `"authenticated"` on our tokens); see Pattern #JWT-Claims below for why. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418020000_us_jwt_app_role_claim.sql` (supersedes the `role`-claim version from `us_009_rls_policies.sql`).
40	- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.

# Ralph Progress Log — Marketify

---

## Codebase Patterns
*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*

- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md §2.
- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji in product UI** — Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
- **One role per account in v1** — no dual-role logic.
- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package — pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules — it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` → `open -a Simulator` → `bunx expo start --ios` in background → `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md §4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration — `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 — no commit-blocking action needed.
- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` — intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. **Note the `app_role` claim (not `role`)** — the bare `role` claim is reserved by PostgREST for the Postgres DB role (always `"authenticated"` on our tokens); see Pattern #JWT-Claims below for why. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418020000_us_jwt_app_role_claim.sql` (supersedes the `role`-claim version from `us_009_rls_policies.sql`).
- **The `submission_reuse_view` → `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions → applications → listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.
